Why does ice float on water? -> Ice floats on water because its density (approximately 0.92 g/cm³) is less than the density of liquid water (approximately 1.0 g/cm³ at STP). According to the principle of buoyancy, an object will float in a fluid if its density is less than the density of the fluid.

How do I change a flat car tire? -> To change a flat car tire, first loosen the lug nuts by hand, then use a jack to lift the vehicle and remove the flat tire. Replace it with the spare tire, tighten the lug nuts, and lower the vehicle safely.

What is the difference between weather and climate? -> Weather refers to short-term atmospheric conditions, such as temperature, humidity, precipitation, and wind, that can change from hour to hour or day to day. Climate refers to the long-term average of weather patterns in a particular area, typically over a period of 30 years or more, as defined by the World Meteorological Organization.
